skill.name: task-runner
skill.description: Discover and run project tasks through the just command runner, including common recipes for tests, builds, linting, and development workflows.
skill.summary: Run project-defined just recipes for tests, builds, linting, and task discovery.
skill.author: 0xDarkMatter

security_audit.summary: Static external command findings are true positives because the skill instructs the assistant to run just recipes through shell commands. The weak cryptographic algorithm findings at SKILL.md:3 and SKILL.md:80 are false positives; both lines contain descriptive text, not cryptographic code. No evidence found of prompt injection, network exfiltration, or confirmed malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Arbitrary Project Recipe Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill directs the assistant to run just commands that execute project-defined recipes. The referenced recipes can run tests, builds, deployment scripts, and cleanup commands, so untrusted repositories could define harmful shell actions.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicitly documented and the skill allows Bash usage. The risk depends on the repository justfile content, so this is a legitimate workflow with a real external command execution hazard.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Match Is False Positive
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged weak cryptographic algorithm patterns at SKILL.md:3 and SKILL.md:80, but those lines are only descriptive task-runner text. No cryptographic function, hash algorithm, or related implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is frontmatter description text and line 80 is a comment about listing recipe descriptions. There is no code path that performs cryptographic operations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Invocation Through just
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill provides Bash examples for running just, just test, just build, just lint, just deploy production, and other project-defined commands. These commands can execute arbitrary shell commands from a justfile.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The documented commands directly invoke an external command runner and include recipes that can remove files or deploy code. The pattern is intentional for this skill but still security relevant.

content.value_statement: Projects often hide commands across package scripts, makefiles, and documentation. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code discover and run just recipes consistently.
actual_capabilities: Checks for available just recipes in a project.
Runs common recipes such as test, build, lint, and dev.
Shows recipe summaries and details with just commands.
Supports running recipes from a specific subdirectory justfile.
Documents common justfile patterns for project automation.
limitations: Requires the just CLI to be installed before use.
Can only run recipes that exist in the current project.
Does not verify that a recipe is safe before execution.
Recipe behavior depends entirely on the repository justfile.

use_cases.title: Onboard to a Repository
use_cases.description: List available just recipes before running project commands.

use_cases.title: Run Quality Checks
use_cases.description: Execute standard test, build, and lint recipes from one interface.

use_cases.title: Standardize Local Automation
use_cases.description: Use documented justfile recipes instead of searching project notes.

prompt_templates.title: List Tasks
prompt_templates.prompt: Check this project for available just recipes and summarize what each one appears to do.

prompt_templates.title: Run Tests
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the appropriate just recipe for tests, review it first, then run it if it looks safe.

prompt_templates.title: Build and Lint
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the justfile to run the project build and lint workflows. Report failures with the relevant command output.

prompt_templates.title: Inspect a Subproject
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the justfile under the backend directory, list its recipes, and recommend which task should validate backend changes.

output_examples.output: The project exposes recipes for tests, builds, linting, development, cleanup, and deployment. Review deployment and cleanup before running them.
The test recipe was selected from the justfile. The result should include whether tests passed and any failing test names.

output_examples.input: Show details for the build recipe.
output_examples.output: The build recipe should be displayed before execution so the user can confirm any package manager or shell commands.

best_practices: List recipes before running commands in a new repository.
Review cleanup, deploy, and script recipes before execution.
Run the narrowest recipe that validates the current change.
anti_patterns: Running deploy recipes without explicit user approval.
Executing recipes from untrusted repositories without inspection.
Assuming just recipes are equivalent across different projects.

faq.question: Does this skill require just?
faq.answer: Yes. The just CLI must be installed before the documented commands can run.

faq.question: Can it create a justfile?
faq.answer: The skill focuses on discovering and running existing recipes. It can guide creation only if asked.

faq.question: Is it safe to run any recipe?
faq.answer: No. Recipes are project-defined shell commands, so sensitive recipes should be reviewed first.

faq.question: Can it run tasks in subdirectories?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents using a specific justfile path for subdirectory workflows.

faq.question: What tools are supported?
faq.answer: The report lists compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What commands does it commonly run?
faq.answer: Common examples include listing recipes, running tests, building, linting, and showing recipe details.
